feed.title: RP Cancer Research Lab @ Loyola University Chicago
feed.subtitle: Welcome to the RP Lab at Loyola University Chicago. We study how long non-coding RNAs (lncRNAs) drive heterogeneity in ER+ breast cancer through their structure and function, and how transient oncogenes and premalignant microenvironments govern the earliest steps of malignant transformation. Our translational research integrates primary patient-derived organoids, mouse models, molecular approaches, and computational biology to connect fundamental discoveries in RNA biology and cancer genetics to novel therapeutic and diagnostic strategies.

entry.title: Dr. Przanowska in The Scientist
entry.content:
Dr. Przanowska was featured in a Postdoc Portrait in The Scientist, discussing her research on long non-coding RNA structures in breast cancer. A wonderful way to mark the close of a chapter of postdoctoral training and the beginning of the next one.

Read the full article in The Scientist
entry.summary: Dr. Przanowska was featured in a Postdoc Portrait in The Scientist, discussing her research on long non-coding RNA structures in breast cancer. A wonderful way to mark the close of a chapter of postdoctoral training and the beginning of the next one.

entry.title: Dr. Przanowski won a gold medal in longsword at Kings Cup 2026

link.title: Dr. Przanowski won a gold medal in longsword at Kings Cup 2026

entry.content:
Dr. Przanowski won a gold medal in Open Longsword and a silver medal in Veterans (40+) at the Kings Cup 2026. Congratulations!

See the epic exchange on YouTube
entry.summary: Dr. Przanowski won a gold medal in Open Longsword and a silver medal in Veterans (40+) at the Kings Cup 2026. Congratulations!
